deviceIdentity.item: Confirm exact device model and variant.
Provide firmware version and supported interface options.
Confirm serial number format and device role in the airport radio system.

snmpAccess.item: Provide SNMP communication details including IP addressing, UDP ports, timeout, retry, and trap destination setup.
Confirm SNMPv2c community or SNMPv3 credential requirements.
Provide SNMP enable procedure for the device or converter-exposed endpoint.
Confirm RS232-to-TCP/IP converter model and whether SNMP is exposed natively or through conversion.

mibOidTrapDefinition.item: Provide full OID list with data types, units, access mode, and polling guidance.
Provide trap definition, trap OIDs, varbind structure, and sample trap payloads.
Confirm whether polling, traps, or both are required for operational integration.

radioOperationalParameters.item: Provide RF parameter list available through SNMP.
Provide input RSSI OID or trap source, value range, units, and thresholds.
Provide output power OID or trap source, value range, units, and thresholds.
Provide channel, frequency, link status, and device health OIDs if available.

alarmMapping.item: For each alarm, provide alarm code, source OID/trap, label, severity, active condition, and clear condition.
Confirm alarm mapping for RF degradation, input RSSI, output power, link failure, hardware fault, and communication fault.

acknowledgementAndClear.item: Confirm whether alarms auto-clear or require manual acknowledgement.
Confirm whether acknowledgement status is exposed through SNMP.
Provide clear alarm behavior and sample clear values or trap payloads.

integrationTesting.item: Provide trap samples for active and cleared alarm states.

oneMapping.item: Map BHE BRTL32 device to ONE asset and airport location hierarchy.
Map SNMP alarms and RF metrics to ONE Alarm Console and LINK normalized events.
Map confirmed radio faults to Work Management recommendations.
Map raw SNMP evidence to Evidence / Reports export fields.

notes: No BHE OIDs, alarm codes, traps, or MIB entries are included because they were not supplied.
Checklist is intended for vendor/customer evidence collection before implementation.
